sources.title: Pearson Edexcel A Level Mathematics 9MA0 specification

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● understand mathematics and mathematical processes in a way that promotes confidence, fosters enjoyment and provides a strong foundation for progress to further study
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r0-b3-l0,p11-r1-b0-l0,p11-r1-b0-l1, OFFICIAL-AIMS-1

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● recognise when mathematics can be used to analyse and solve a problem in context
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r10-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-10

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● represent situations mathematically and understand the relationship between problems in context and mathematical models that may be applied to solve them
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r10-b1-l0,p11-r11-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-11

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● draw diagrams and sketch graphs to help explore mathematical situations and interpret solutions
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r12-b0-l0,p11-r13-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-12

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● make deductions and inferences and draw conclusions by using mathematical reasoning
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r14-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-13

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● interpret solutions and communicate their interpretation effectively in the context of the problem
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r14-b1-l0,p11-r15-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-14

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● read and comprehend mathematical arguments, including justifications of methods and formulae, and communicate their understanding
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r16-b0-l0,p11-r17-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-15

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● read and comprehend articles concerning applications of mathematics and communicate their understanding
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r18-b0-l0,p11-r19-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-16

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● use technology such as calculators and computers effectively and recognise when their use may be inappropriate
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r20-b0-l0,p11-r21-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-17

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● take increasing responsibility for their own learning and the evaluation of their own mathematical development.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r22-b0-l0,p11-r23-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-18

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● extend their range of mathematical skills and techniques
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 11, line IDs p11-r0-b2-l0,p11-r2-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-AIMS-2

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● understand coherence and progression in mathematics and how different areas of mathematics are connected

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● apply mathematics in other fields of study and be aware of the relevance of mathematics to the world of work and to situations in society in general

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● use their mathematical knowledge to make logical and reasoned decisions in solving problems both within pure mathematics and in a variety of contexts, and communicate the mathematical rationale for these decisions clearly

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● generalise mathematically

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● construct mathematical proofs

statements.statementText: Pearson has provided a large data set, which will support the assessment of Statistics in Paper 3: Statistics and Mechanics. Students are required to become familiar with the data set in advance of the final assessment.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 14, line IDs p14-r0-b1-l0,p14-r0-b1-l1,p14-r0-b1-l2, OFFICIAL-9MA0-LDS-1

reviewNotes: Familiarity with a prescribed large data set directly matches this node.

statements.statementText: Assessments will be designed in such a way that questions assume knowledge and understanding of the data set. The expectation is that these questions should be likely to give a material advantage to students who have studied and are familiar with the data set.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 14, line IDs p14-r0-b2-l0,p14-r0-b2-l1,p14-r0-b2-l2, OFFICIAL-9MA0-LDS-2

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● assume familiarity with the terminology and contexts of the data, and do not explain them in a way that gives students who have not studied the data set the same opportunities to access marks as students who have studied them

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● use summary statistics or selected data from, or statistical diagrams based on, the data set – these might be provided in the question or task, or as stimulus materials
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 14, line IDs p14-r0-b2-l3,p14-r2-b0-l0,p14-r3-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-9MA0-LDS-4

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● are based on samples related to the contexts in the data set, where students’ work with the data set will help them understand the background context and/or
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 14, line IDs p14-r0-b2-l3,p14-r4-b0-l0,p14-r5-b0-l0, OFFICIAL-9MA0-LDS-5

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● require students to interpret data in ways that would be too demanding in an unfamiliar context.

statements.statementText: Students will not be required to have copies of the data set in the examination, nor will they be required to have detailed knowledge of the actual data within the data set.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 14, line IDs p14-r8-b0-l0,p14-r8-b0-l1, OFFICIAL-9MA0-LDS-7

statements.statementText: The data set can be downloaded from our website, qualifications.pearson.com. This data set should be appropriate for the lifetime of the qualification. However we will review the data set on an annual basis to ensure it is appropriate. If we need to make changes to the data set, we will notify centres before the beginning of the two-year course before students complete their examination.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 14, line IDs p14-r8-b1-l0,p14-r8-b1-l1,p14-r8-b1-l2,p14-r8-b1-l3,p14-r8-b1-l4, OFFICIAL-9MA0-LDS-8

statements.statementText: Understand and use the structure of mathematical proof, proceeding from given assumptions through a series of logical steps to a conclusion; use methods of proof, including: Proof by deduction Proof by exhaustion Disproof by counter example Proof by contradiction (including proof of the irrationality of 2 and the infinity of primes, and application to unfamiliar proofs).
examplesText: Proof by deduction: for example, complete the square to prove n² − 6n + 10 is positive for all n.
Proof by exhaustion: if p is prime and 3 < p < 25, prove that (p − 1)(p + 1) is a multiple of 12.
Disproof by counterexample: show that ‘n² − n + 1 is prime for all n’ is untrue.

reviewNotes: Exact match for proof by deduction method.
Exact match for proof by exhaustion method.
Exact match for disproof by counterexample method.
Exact match for proof by contradiction method.
